tariff_system: East African Community Common External Tariff (EAC CET)
summary: Kenya uses 8-digit HS codes aligned with East African Community (EAC) Common External Tariff. Kenya is part of the EAC Customs Union (Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Rwanda, Burundi) with harmonized tariff schedules.

sources.name: EAC Common External Tariff Portal
sources.description: East African Community Online Customs Tariff database

sources.name: EAC CET 2022 Version (Updated June 2025)
sources.description: Latest EAC Common External Tariff version with June 2025 updates

sources.name: Kenya Revenue Authority - EAC CET 2022
sources.description: Kenya's published EAC CET 2022 version with all tariff lines and duties

sources.name: EAC CET Sections & Documents
sources.description: EAC Common External Tariff documentation and updates

sources.name: SEAIR HS Code Database - Kenya
sources.description: Kenyan HS codes and trade data

sources.name: Kenya Tariff Schedule - World Bank WITS
sources.description: World Bank's tariff database with Kenyan schedules

sources.name: EAC AfCFTA Tariff Concessions Schedule
sources.description: EAC provisional tariff schedule for African Continental Free Trade Area

special_provisions.description: Special fifth column in tariff schedule for Sensitive Items (SI)
special_provisions.note: Sensitive items have higher tariff bands

next_steps: Download EAC CET 2022 (June 2025 version) from KRA or EABC
Extract all HS codes and duty rates from tariff schedule
Note special provisions for Sensitive Items
Cross-reference with AfCFTA schedule for preferential rates
notes: Kenya uses EAC harmonized tariff system. Best source is Kenya Revenue Authority or EAC Secretariat. Most current version is June 2025 update of 2022 CET. Duty structure: 0% (raw materials), 10% (intermediate), 25% (finished goods), 35% (sensitive items). AfCFTA tariff schedule available for trade within African Continental Free Trade Area.
